basic_info.incident_name: BP Texas City Refinery Explosion and Fire
basic_info.incident_name_cn: 英国石油公司德克萨斯城炼油厂爆炸火灾事故

process_context.operating_conditions: Atmospheric pressure, elevated temperature (~140°C), during startup after turnaround

failure_mechanism.root_cause: Overfilling of the raffinate splitter tower due to inadequate level indication, operator misjudgment, and lack of independent high-level protection, leading to hydrocarbon overflow into blowdown drum and subsequent overpressure and rupture.
failure_mechanism.causal_chain: Inadequate maintenance of level instrumentation → Incorrect level reading during startup → Operator failed to recognize overfill → Tower overflowed liquid into blowdown drum → Drum overpressurized and ruptured → Hydrocarbon vapor cloud formed → Ignition → Massive explosion and fire
failure_mechanism.physical_principles: Hydrostatic head, vapor-liquid equilibrium, two-phase flow in relief systems, deflagration-to-detonation transition in confined vapor clouds

protection_system.safeguard_failures: Level transmitter was faulty and not calibrated; glass gauge obscured by residue and misread; relief valves undersized and not designed for liquid carryover; PSM lacked effective management of change and mechanical integrity verification
csb_recommendations: Implement independent high-integrity level shutdown systems (SIL-2 or higher) for critical towers
Require rigorous mechanical integrity verification prior to process startup after turnaround
Strengthen regulatory oversight of process safety culture and metrics by OSHA and EPA

embedding_text: During startup of the ISOM unit raffinate splitter tower at BP Texas City refinery, a faulty level instrument and operator error caused severe overfilling with C5-C6 hydrocarbons. Liquid overflowed into the blowdown drum, exceeding its design capacity and causing catastrophic overpressure and rupture. This released a large flammable vapor cloud that ignited, resulting in a massive explosion killing 15 and injuring 180. The root cause was failure of multiple layers of protection including instrumentation, procedures, and safety culture.
